name: developer-growth-analysis
description: Analyzes your recent Claude Code chat history to identify coding patterns, development gaps, and areas for improvement, curates relevant learning resources from HackerNews, and automatically sends a personalized growth report to your Slack DMs.
capabilities: Reads and analyzes Claude Code chat history from the past 24-48 hours
Identifies development patterns including technologies, problem types, and challenges
Detects specific skill gaps with evidence-based recommendations
Curates HackerNews articles tailored to each improvement area
Generates comprehensive growth reports with action items
Sends personalized reports directly to your Slack DMs
author: DYAI2025
securitySummary: All 46 static findings are false positives. The skill is a prompt-based documentation file containing only instructions for analyzing chat history, curating learning resources, and sending reports via MCP tools. No executable code, scripts, or direct network/filesystem operations exist in the skill itself. All operations are mediated through Claude Code's built-in tools and Rube MCP integrations.
valueStatement: You want to improve as a developer but do not know where to focus. This skill analyzes your Claude Code chat history to identify patterns, skill gaps, and improvement areas, then curates HackerNews learning resources and delivers everything to your Slack.
limitations: Requires access to local chat history file (~/.claude/history.jsonl)
Depends on Rube MCP for HackerNews search and Slack integration
Analysis is limited to conversations within Claude Code interface
Cannot access work done outside of Claude Code sessions

useCases.title: Weekly Growth Review
useCases.description: Get a weekly summary of your development work, identify skill gaps, and receive curated learning resources.

useCases.title: Team Pattern Analysis
useCases.description: Understand common challenges across your team by analyzing individual developer chat patterns.

useCases.title: Skill Gap Identification
useCases.description: Discover specific areas to focus on when transitioning to a new technology or domain.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze my developer growth from my recent chats

promptTemplates.title: Today's Focus
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze my work from today and suggest areas for improvement

promptTemplates.title: Custom Period
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze my coding patterns from the past week and identify my top 3 improvement areas

promptTemplates.title: Full Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Give me a complete developer growth report including work summary, strengths, weaknesses, and learning resources from HackerNews

bestPractices: Run analysis weekly to track improvement trajectory over time
Focus on one improvement area before moving to the next
Apply learning resources to actual work projects for maximum retention
antiPatterns: Running analysis too frequently reduces actionable insights
Ignoring identified patterns and not acting on recommendations
Skipping the curated resources and relying only on generic tutorials

faq.question: What data does this skill access?
faq.answer: The skill reads your local Claude Code chat history file which contains your messages, projects, and timestamps.

faq.question: How far back does the analysis go?
faq.answer: By default, the skill analyzes chat history from the past 24-48 hours based on the current timestamp.

faq.question: Does this integrate with other tools?
faq.answer: Yes, it uses Rube MCP for HackerNews searches and Slack message delivery. Both require separate authentication.

faq.question: Is my chat data stored or shared?
faq.answer: Chat data is analyzed locally and only shared with you via Slack DM. No data is stored by the skill itself.

faq.question: Why is my report not showing?
faq.answer: Ensure Slack is connected via Rube MCP and you have recent Claude Code chat history. The skill requires both MCP tools to function.

faq.question: How is this different from code review?
faq.answer: This skill analyzes your development patterns and habits across sessions, focusing on skill gaps and learning, while code review focuses on specific code quality.
